title: Comment on: Short pulse width widens the therapeutic window of subthalamic neurostimulation
journal_name: Annals of Clinical and Translational Neurology
study_question: Does short pulse width widen therapeutic window in subthalamic neurostimulation?
population_assessed: Small number of patients receiving subthalamic deep brain stimulation
outcome_measures: Charge per pulse, therapeutic window, rigidity control, adverse muscle contractions
answer_to_study_question: Wider pulses result in larger therapeutic window when measured by charge per pulse, not current.
